entries.garrytan/gbrain: This project functions as an opinionated brain for Hermes Agent, utilizing a self-wiring knowledge graph with automated entity and relationship extraction. It offers a sophisticated hybrid search mechanism and includes 26 modular skills to manage data ingestion and enrichment.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It is designed for seamless integration with major LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness accumulates validated knowledge and artifacts across repeated agent runs to improve performance on complex tasks. It utilizes a multi-agent loop to evaluate strategies and gate which knowledge is persisted for future missions.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG-based engine to ensure no message data is ever deleted. It features an immutable-first SQLite storage system and provides specialized tools for expanding and searching compacted history.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This backend enables private, cross-session memory persistence through a self-hosted architecture. It supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server and includes automated setup scripts for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first, on-device memory engine that uses a unified SQLite vault to maintain persistence without cloud dependencies. It employs hybrid retrieval via vector search, BM25, and graph traversal to automate decision capture and handoff generation.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur provides a shared memory layer for multi-agent systems using an open-format YAML engram structure. The system features zero API costs and implements automatic memory decay and strengthening based on how frequently information is used.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system uses an MCP-native server to prefetch context by monitoring agent session logs. It is a local-first solution utilizing SQLite-vec and GGUF models to provide proactive RAG capabilities.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks across 26 domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a tiered 109-entry word replacement table. It ensures Hermes's output remains human-like through a two-pass audit process compatible with Claude Code and OpenClaw.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular cross-platform library that grants agents capabilities in architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation and full compatibility with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on type safety. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token usage during skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate six types of professional diagrams, including UML and ML architectures, from natural language. It includes a five-round self-check safety valve to iteratively refine visual layouts and coordinate snapping.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Designed for blockchain interoperability, this skill enables Hermes to perform cross-chain sends via CCIP and monitor multi-chain data feeds. It follows the standardized agentskills.io spec to facilitate secure smart contract interactions.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming tool allows Hermes to weave source code and prose into unified .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between documentation and source files.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with shared memory across instances using markdown-based fabric directories. It automates the extraction of decision-quality training data to help the agent fine-tune its own replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: This registry manages the lifecycle of reusable AI skills through an OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI. It uniquely supports skill monetization via Stripe and the TON blockchain while handling automated dependency resolution.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX image generation, providing Hermes with precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. It supports structured JSON prompting and exact hex color specifications for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities via slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness for long-running tasks that utilizes structured mission planning and JSON snapshots. It enables Hermes to manage complex features through a plan-approve-execute workflow with native handoff briefs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints for real-time progress tracking. It ensures reliability by providing rollback procedures for safe recovery during complex operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize context token consumption. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading of configurations.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This forge converts repositories and developer discourse into compliant AI skills by mapping instructions to specific source code lines. it allows Hermes to generate version-pinned instruction sets for various software libraries.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and installing skills that features automated security scanning for prompt injection. It supports micropayment protocols for premium content and includes an agent-to-agent feedback system with trust scores.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem using the C# MCP SDK. It supports automatic skill discovery and progressive disclosure to maintain efficiency in C#-based agent environments.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages and flakes for a wide array of AI coding agents, including Hermes. It is ideal for developers who want to execute tools directly via Nix commands without manual installation or environment management.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw offers a high-security deployment environment using hardware-enforced sandboxing via NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking. It features pre-configured security policies and an integrated library of over 40 tools, making it the primary choice for safety-critical agent execution.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services for fully reproducible deployments. It prioritizes security through sops-nix integration and supports a hybrid skill model for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this portable desktop agent requires no installation, admin rights, or Docker to run over 100 integrated tools. It features a GUI for local model management and a workflow engine for scheduling automated, conditional AI tasks.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This repository provides a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using simple build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Focused on production-ready headless operation, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is specifically designed for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integration with the Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This Docker Compose stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based interaction. It is optimized for Portainer users, offering automated image builds via GitHub Actions for easy deployment as a Git Repository stack.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages complex multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users requiring strict operational oversight, featuring the Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off and integrated spend monitoring.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms that leverage durable memory and Model Context Protocol (MCP) support. It stands out by enabling agents to learn new skills directly from conversations and supports 23 different model providers for flexible delegation.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to refine the harness code used for evaluating Hermes Agent. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation to improve agent efficiency.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent and Paperclip, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates 17 specialized agents across the entire development lifecycle, from initial research and planning to infrastructure deployment. It ensures high-quality outputs by enforcing mandatory quality gates for code reviews and testing at every phase of the project.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle (SDLC) by providing Hermes Agent with structural code graphs via Supermodel MCP. It automates an eight-phase workflow that enforces architectural integrity through graph-diff phase gates, ensuring code quality from planning to health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: Focusing on safe delegation, this project routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It implements strict safety controls, including configurable timeouts and character limits, to maintain isolated and predictable delegation workflows.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A Rust-based CLI tool that monitors token consumption across over 20 AI coding agents and CLIs. It provides real-time cost tracking via LiteLLM data and features a unique 3D visualization dashboard for usage analysis.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This project offers community-driven visual customizations for the Hermes terminal interface. Users can define custom ASCII banners, spinners, and color schemes using YAML-based configurations that allow for partial theme overrides.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A specialized port that integrates the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel. It runs natively without OAuth or external processes and includes integrated MLOps tools for inference and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It enables durable session management with checkpoints and provides a supervisory sidebar for human auditing and intervention.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A framework for building Task-Agnostic Workflow (TAW) agents that operate within specific project scopes. It allows for per-folder configurations, enabling seamless switching between default and scoped agents with local memories and tools.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A zero-latency static linter designed to identify structural bugs in AI agent configurations and system prompts. It uses seven specialized detectors to provide automated quality gating for CI/CD pipelines without requiring LLM calls or API keys.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A dedicated migration utility that facilitates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the mapping of model configurations, personas, and memories, while also handling the transfer of local and system-level credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: An automated deployment tool that spins up a complete Hermes Agent stack using Docker, LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It provides a zero-cost environment featuring 29 community plugins and pre-configured routing for free local models.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build web interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI through a streamlined three-panel layout for chat, file browsing, and session management. It is designed for accessibility across both desktop and mobile browsers using a lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript architecture.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A security-hardened native web workspace that features real-time SSE streaming for agent outputs and tool call rendering. It offers comprehensive administrative control over agent memory, skills, and background jobs, protected by execution approval prompts and authentication middleware.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard acts as a multi-agent gateway for configuring and monitoring multiple agent profiles simultaneously. It distinguishes itself with detailed token analytics that track costs by model and platform alongside a browser-based terminal and system metrics panel.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that establishes direct SSH connections to agents without requiring middle-layer APIs or remote daemons. It provides a multi-tabbed terminal environment for managing remote cron jobs, skills, and SQLite session stores directly from the desktop.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This React-based Telegram Mini App offers a mobile-first dashboard featuring a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It enables users to spawn background agents and monitor live output alongside integrated system health gauges for CPU and memory usage.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app focused on simplifying the lifecycle of Hermes Agent through guided installation and automated dependency resolution. It supports a wide array of LLM providers and includes integrated messaging gateways for platforms like Discord and Telegram.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: This monitoring-centric dashboard specializes in process tracking and cost distribution analysis for Hermes sessions. It features deep conversation inspection powered by FTS5 search and provides a read-only interface for viewing configurations and scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that integrates a marketplace for skills, MCP servers, and plugins to extend agent capabilities. It utilizes isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate while providing a visual timeline of tool executions.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Featuring a glassmorphic design, this single-file HTML interface uses a Python proxy to deliver real-time previews of HTML, SVG, and PDF artifacts. It combines a modern aesthetic with functional tools for inspecting local agent memory and file directories.
